article_title: OpenAI Robotics Chief Caitlin Kalinowski Resigns Over Pentagon Deal, Citing Insufficient Safeguards on Surveillance and Autonomous Weapons
summary: Duplicate of already-published article on the same topic (PR #195, now published as 09-openais-robotics-hardware-lead-resigns-over-pentagon-deal)

content_preview.title: OpenAI Robotics Chief Caitlin Kalinowski Resigns Over Pentagon Deal, Citing Insufficient Safeguards on Surveillance and Autonomous Weapons
content_preview.summary: Kalinowski becomes the most senior OpenAI employee to publicly oppose the Pentagon contract, warning that domestic surveillance and lethal autonomy deserved more deliberation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Caitlin Kalinowski, who led robotics and hardware operations at OpenAI, resigned in early March 2026 over the company's agreement to deploy AI models on the Pentagon's classified computing networks. Kalinowski is the most senior OpenAI employee to publicly oppose the deal, which was signed on February 27 and drew immediate criticism for its timing and rollout.

Her departure adds an internal dimension to a controversy that has already triggered a consumer boycott, a surge in ChatGPT...

This article covers the exact same event (Caitlin Kalinowski's resignation from OpenAI over the Pentagon deal) as PR #195, which was already approved and published as '09-openais-robotics-hardware-lead-resigns-over-pentagon-deal-citing-rushed-guardrails-on-surveillance-and-autonomous-weapons'. Same core story, same key quotes, same sources (NPR, Fortune), no new information or angle.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Rejected as duplicate. The article was produced by a parallel agent that independently chose the same topic as another agent. The first submission (PR #195) has already been published.
